task_completion.description: The OpenBCI GUI is running an active Synthetic EEG session.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI application window is visible, and there are active, moving waveforms (like the Time Series) indicating a running session.

task_completion.description: The GUI displays a multi-panel layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The interface is divided into multiple distinct widget panels (e.g., a grid of different graphs or visualizations).

task_completion.description: At least one widget panel displays the FFT Plot visualization.
task_completion.visual_evidence: One of the panels has the title 'FFT Plot' and shows a frequency spectrum graph.

task_completion.description: The FFT Plot shows the x-axis representing frequency (in Hz) and the y-axis showing signal amplitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FFT Plot panel has an x-axis labeled with frequency values (Hz) and a y-axis labeled with amplitude values (e.g., uV).

task_completion.description: The channels in the FFT Plot are color-coded to match the Time Series display.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The lines in the FFT Plot are drawn in multiple distinct colors (e.g., red, green, blue, purple) corresponding to the channel colors seen in the Time Series widget.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the standard OpenBCI GUI interface with its typical menus, buttons, and window borders, rather than a text editor, image editor, or web browser mockup.

integrity.description: The FFT plot shows actual dynamic synthetic EEG data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The FFT Plot contains complex, jagged lines representing real-time frequency data, not a blank graph or a static, perfectly smooth placeholder image.

integrity.description: The agent configured the widget via the GUI rather than just printing a success message in the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal does not show the agent simply echoing a success message; the final state must show the actual GUI with the FFT Plot visible.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task operates in OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2. The initial setup deliberately removes the default FFT Plot widget by replacing it with a Head Plot widget. The agent must successfully navigate the UI to add the FFT Plot back into one of the widget panels.
